_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
clinical_indications: Traumatic injuries requiring pressure relief on brain
Traumatic injuries requiring drainage of blood from the brain
Traumatic injuries requiring removal of shrapnel or other objects lodged in the skull
To implant a deep brain stimulation device
To introduce an endoscope to assist in tumor evacuation or aneurysm treatment
To obtain a tissue sample for a biopsy

ppd_preauth.q: Clinical notes - detailed history, signs & symptoms, planned line of treatment, indication for procedure?
Was Glasgow coma scale and imaging indicative of surgery?

cpd_claim.q: Are the detailed ICPs with daily vitals and treatment details?
Are the detailed procedure / Operative Notes available?
Is the Discharge summary with follow-up advise at the time of discharge?
Was the imaging indicative of surgery?

common_queries: Clinical notes, history, signs & symptoms, planned line of treatment, or indication for procedure is missing or incomplete
Imaging did not confirm the diagnosis
GCS and/or imaging not indicative of surgery
Detailed Indoor Case Papers (ICPs) missing or lacking daily vitals and treatment details
Detailed Procedure / Operative Notes not available or incomplete
Discharge summary missing or lacking follow-up advice

clinical_key_pointers: A burr hole drainage may be used to treat conditions caused by a buildup of fluid around the brain: Subdural or Epidural hematoma, Glasgow coma scale score <8, Hydrocephalus, Brain tumor.

Burr holes as part of emergency procedures resulting from traumatic injuries: Relieve pressure on brain, Drain blood from the brain after a traumatic injury, Remove shrapnel or other objects lodged in the skull.

A burr hole may be employed to assist in the treatment of conditions: To implant a deep brain stimulation device, To introduce an endoscope—small medical scope—to assist in tumor evacuation or aneurysm treatment, To obtain a tissue sample for a biopsy.
